expectations.text: Clarifies that heavy lifting itself is unlikely to directly prevent fat loss — energy balance is the main driver
expectations.evidence: The answer argues heavy lifting creates conditions that defend body weight through 'Elevated Metabolic Demand & Adaptive Thermogenesis,' elevated hunger hormones, and cortisol. Does not clearly state the hosts' position that heavy lifting itself is unlikely to directly prevent fat loss.

expectations.text: Notes that switching from volume work to frequent 1RM attempts doesn't substantially change calorie expenditure
expectations.evidence: No discussion of calorie expenditure differences between volume and 1RM training approaches.

expectations.text: Suggests or implies the coaching setup may be suboptimal (years of training with limited progress)
expectations.evidence: Does not question the coaching setup or suggest the trainer may be poor. The answer addresses the listener's situation without critiquing the coaching arrangement.

expectations.text: Mentions bodyweight performance targets (push-ups, pull-ups, dips) as a motivational strategy during weight loss
expectations.evidence: No mention of bodyweight performance targets as motivational tools during weight loss.

expectations.text: Recommends professional clinical support (dietitian or similar) for binge-eating concerns rather than just programming changes
expectations.evidence: 'Consider working with a therapist or eating disorder specialist' under point 4 of the recommendations.
